title: Running With The Bulls

jokes.text: Larry's virgin counting obsession in his dream - concerned about missing the 72nd virgin while 71 beautiful ones want him
jokes.setup: Larry describes having a dream with 71 virgins who want to have sex with him
jokes.punchline: He's too preoccupied with finding the missing 72nd virgin to enjoy any of them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David character moment - taking a fantasy scenario and making it neurotic. The absurd priorities reveal his obsessive personality beautifully.

jokes.text: Dr. Templeton: 'You are the first and only patient who has ever said anything negative about that chair'
jokes.setup: Larry complains extensively about the uncomfortable chair
jokes.punchline: Therapist deadpans that Larry is literally the only patient to ever complain
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that highlights Larry's unique neurosis. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Larry offers to go chair shopping with his therapist
jokes.setup: Larry has been complaining about the chair situation
jokes.punchline: Suggests he'll personally help his therapist shop for better chairs
jokes.explanation: Escalates the chair complaint into an absurd offer. Sets up the callback that pays off later.

jokes.text: Sneaky watch peek conversation - Larry calls out the therapist's time-checking
jokes.setup: Therapist checks his watch to end the session
jokes.punchline: Larry confronts him about the 'sneaky watch peek' and suggests putting a clock behind the patient
jokes.explanation: Another Larry observation about social awkwardness. 'Sneaky watch peek' is a memorable phrase.

jokes.text: Larry: 'No, I loathe them. They're disgusting.' about truffles after Dr. Templeton gushes about truffle season
jokes.setup: Therapist enthusiastically describes truffle season
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt, unfiltered disgust
jokes.explanation: Larry's inability to politely agree or deflect - just pure honest disgust delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Larry tells therapist 'any time you wanna get rid of me as a patient, just say I've had enough'
jokes.setup: End of therapy session
jokes.punchline: Larry gives his therapist permission to fire him as a patient
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry self-awareness about being difficult. Subverts normal patient-therapist dynamic.

jokes.text: Doesn't cost you a nickel... She's excited to be with me. She wants to please me.
jokes.setup: Continuing explanation of the dating scheme
jokes.punchline: Larry's gleeful description of the power dynamic advantages
jokes.explanation: Larry's delight in exploiting the professional relationship dynamic is character-specific cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Richard Lewis's outfit looks like 'the Little Drummer Boy's funeral - this is what he's wearing in the casket'
jokes.setup: Richard Lewis is dressed pretentiously at his art show
jokes.punchline: Larry's specific visual comparison to funeral attire for a Christmas character
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and visual insult. The Christmas/funeral juxtaposition is unexpectedly perfect.

jokes.text: Richard Lewis's delusional self-portrait - paints himself as he looked 50 years ago but insists it's current
jokes.setup: Lewis shows Larry a painting he claims is a current self-portrait
jokes.punchline: Larry points out it looks like him from 50 years ago, Lewis insists it's accurate
jokes.explanation: Perfect character delusion moment. Lewis's vanity creates the comedy through self-deception.

jokes.text: That's you? What do you think a self portrait means?... That's like you from 50 years ago!
jokes.setup: Richard shows Larry his self-portrait painting
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutal honesty about how unrealistic the self-portrait is
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry-Richard dynamic - Larry's inability to lie about Richard's delusions.

jokes.text: Cheryl questions Larry's 'two and two' logic - 'I don't see the two and two'
jokes.setup: Larry claims he's putting 'two and two together' about Kenny's death
jokes.punchline: Cheryl literally doesn't see what the 'two and two' are in his speculation
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that highlights the absurdity of Larry's reasoning through literal interpretation of the idiom.

jokes.text: Larry: 'You're running with the bulls. It'd be shocking if he didn't die.'
jokes.setup: Everyone is shocked that Kenny died running with bulls
jokes.punchline: Larry points out that death is the expected outcome of bull running
jokes.explanation: Dark but logical observation delivered with Larry's characteristic inappropriate timing and bluntness.

jokes.text: Larry: 'Yeah, but she's not a prostitute' / 'But you weren't paying her for it' explaining the difference between Cheryl and a prostitute
jokes.setup: Cheryl compares herself to the prostitute Kenny was trying to impress
jokes.punchline: Larry clinically explains the transactional difference
jokes.explanation: Larry's matter-of-fact clarification about payment in front of his wife creates perfect awkwardness.

jokes.text: Usually you don't have to impress a prostitute
jokes.setup: Discussion about men doing crazy things to impress women
jokes.punchline: Larry notes that prostitutes are transactional, not impressed by stunts
jokes.explanation: Perfectly logical but inappropriate observation. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Larry asks 'do they throw in the balls?' about gender reassignment surgery
jokes.setup: Discussion of transition surgery timing
jokes.punchline: Larry's crudely practical question about surgical package deals
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate but characteristically blunt Larry question. The casual vulgarity in sensitive context creates cringe.

jokes.text: Dinner? It's 3:30... Hey, you better be at the memorial.
jokes.setup: Larry and Cheryl claim they have an early dinner
jokes.punchline: Marty's incredulity at calling 3:30 'dinner' followed by mild threat
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy about the absurdity of having 'dinner' at 3:30 PM.

jokes.text: Larry brings up the chair disparity with Cheryl, she's never noticed chairs during therapy
jokes.setup: Larry asks Cheryl about the therapist's chair situation
jokes.punchline: Cheryl has never even thought about chairs during therapy
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Larry's obsession vs normal person's priorities. Highlights his unique fixations.

jokes.text: Dr. Templeton connection to truffle season - Larry realizes the coincidence
jokes.setup: Cheryl mentions going to restaurant, Larry mentions truffle season
jokes.punchline: Larry realizes Dr. Templeton told him about truffle season
jokes.explanation: Connects earlier truffle mention, building the running theme. Sets up later confidentiality breach.

jokes.text: Short fly penis problem - can't get penis out of pants with small fly
jokes.setup: Larry struggling with pants
jokes.punchline: Fly is too small to extract penis for bathroom use
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy setup. Slightly crude but establishes the pants issue that becomes plot point.

jokes.text: Leon's tiddlywinks penis technique - 'press down on the shaft, allows your head to pop up'
jokes.setup: Larry's penis extraction problem
jokes.punchline: Leon demonstrates specific technique using tiddlywinks analogy
jokes.explanation: Creative analogy and Leon's helpful but crude advice. 'Tiddlywinks' comparison is unexpectedly perfect.

jokes.text: Leon's pants theory - manufactured in country with small penises vs big penises
jokes.setup: Discussion of why some pants have small flies
jokes.punchline: Geographic penis size determines pants manufacturing specifications
jokes.explanation: Leon's unique worldview creates absurd but internally logical theory. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Leon's toilet water penis story - 'my johnson hangs in the water'
jokes.setup: Leon explaining his big penis advantages
jokes.punchline: Claims it hangs in toilet water when sitting
jokes.explanation: Leon's characteristic bragging escalated to absurd level. Gets laugh but relies on crude shock value.

jokes.text: Richard Lewis always takes the good seat at restaurants - Larry strategizes arriving early
jokes.setup: Larry complains Lewis always gets the better seat
jokes.punchline: Plans to arrive increasingly early to beat him to good seat
jokes.explanation: Sets up the restaurant confrontation. Shows Larry's competitive nature over trivial matters.

jokes.text: Jeff's open house sex revelation - had sex in every house for sale in Brentwood
jokes.setup: Jeff's fake house hunting dates
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's had sex in every available house including one in escrow
jokes.explanation: Escalates the dating scheme to absurd levels. Jeff's shamelessness creates comedy through excess.

jokes.text: Jeff: 'And let's say I'm driving around a different neighborhood and I see a house that's for sale, gets me kind of horny.'
jokes.setup: Jeff explaining his real estate sexual strategy
jokes.punchline: For Sale signs as aphrodisiac
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd sexual conditioning - real estate signs as turn-on is both unexpected and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Leon eavesdropping and wanting house location - 'Tell me where that fucking house is at'
jokes.setup: Leon was supposed to leave but stayed to listen
jokes.punchline: Demands to know where the sex house is located
jokes.explanation: Leon's opportunism and directness. His immediate interest in the practical application is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Patient-doctor vs doctor-patient confidentiality distinction
jokes.setup: Larry mentions breach of patient-doctor confidentiality
jokes.punchline: Dr. Templeton corrects that only doctor-patient confidentiality exists, not the reverse
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that makes a real distinction. The technical correction undermines Larry's complaint perfectly.

jokes.text: Circle of trust demonstration with therapist's arms
jokes.setup: Dr. Templeton wants to demonstrate circle of trust concept
jokes.punchline: Larry refuses to participate, therapist does it alone with his own arms
jokes.explanation: Physical awkwardness as therapist tries new-age demonstration. Larry's refusal makes it more awkward.

jokes.text: Breakthrough argument - Larry insists it's not a breakthrough while therapist writes it down
jokes.setup: Therapist declares Larry's apology is a breakthrough
jokes.punchline: Larry repeatedly denies it's a breakthrough while therapist writes 'breakthrough' anyway
jokes.explanation: Escalating argument over therapeutic interpretation. Larry's resistance to being analyzed creates comedy.

jokes.text: Tiddlywinks callback in therapy - therapist knows the same penis technique
jokes.setup: Larry describes short fly problem to therapist
jokes.punchline: Therapist knows exact same tiddlywinks technique Leon taught
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that connects scenes. The universal knowledge of this technique is absurdly perfect.

jokes.text: The tiddlywink! Yeah! That might work. My friend told me the same thing.
jokes.setup: Therapist suggests same penis extraction technique as Leon
jokes.punchline: Larry's excitement at getting same advice from therapist as from Leon
jokes.explanation: Nice callback connecting Leon's street wisdom with professional therapy advice.

jokes.text: Barneys Warehouse sale mention - therapist casually recommends it to Larry
jokes.setup: Discussion of Larry's pants problems
jokes.punchline: Therapist suggests Barneys Warehouse sale for better pants
jokes.explanation: Plants the seed for later revelation about confidentiality breach. Seems innocent now but becomes important.

jokes.text: Clock behind patient revelation - Larry sees therapist installed the suggested clock
jokes.setup: Larry had suggested putting clock behind patient
jokes.punchline: Therapist actually implemented Larry's suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier suggestion. Shows therapist actually listened to Larry's advice.

jokes.text: Restaurant early arrival backfire - Lewis still beats Larry despite being 20 minutes early
jokes.setup: Larry arrives 20 minutes early to beat Lewis to good seat
jokes.punchline: Lewis was already there 10 minutes before Larry, making Larry 30 minutes early for nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and subversion. Larry's plan backfires because Lewis is equally petty and competitive.

jokes.text: Escalating early arrival threats - ending with Lewis sleeping in kitchen because restaurant bought his painting
jokes.setup: Both threaten increasingly early arrival times
jokes.punchline: Lewis claims he'll sleep in kitchen due to restaurant connection from art purchase
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that callbacks the art sale. The absurdity of sleeping in restaurant kitchen to get good seat is perfect.

jokes.text: Sex in every house in Brentwood expansion - even houses in escrow
jokes.setup: Jeff's open house sex adventures
jokes.punchline: Claims to have had sex in every house for sale, even ones in escrow
jokes.explanation: Escalates the earlier revelation. Jeff's shameless bragging about sexual exploits in real estate.

jokes.text: Gets me kind of horny... She is not only a good-looking woman, but she's so stylish.
jokes.setup: Jeff describing seeing houses for sale
jokes.punchline: Real estate signs as sexual stimulus and shallow attraction reasoning
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate horniness but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: House surprise for Susie cover story - Larry claims he was surprising her with house purchase
jokes.setup: Susie catches Larry with another woman in car
jokes.punchline: Larry claims it was house hunting to surprise Susie with purchase
jokes.explanation: Quick thinking cover story that actually works. Larry's ability to improvise believable lies under pressure.

jokes.text: Memorial seat bribery - Larry pays usher $20 to reserve seat near exit
jokes.setup: Larry wants specific seat at memorial service
jokes.punchline: Bribes usher with increasing amounts to reserve exit seat due to fatwa fears
jokes.explanation: Larry's paranoia leads to inappropriate behavior at memorial. The bribery shows his priority of safety over respect.

jokes.text: Dr. Templeton funeral nod explanation - 'downward nod indicating solemn hello vs upward nod for joyous occasion'
jokes.setup: Larry questions therapist's greeting at funeral
jokes.punchline: Detailed technical explanation of funeral vs regular greeting protocols
jokes.explanation: Brilliant over-analysis of simple social interaction. The technical precision applied to basic greeting is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Barneys Warehouse confidentiality standoff - Larry refuses to reveal source despite mounting pressure
jokes.setup: Everyone suspicious about how Larry knew about Barneys sale
jokes.punchline: Larry dramatically refuses to reveal confidential source under any circumstances
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of trivial information into dramatic standoff. Larry's commitment to confidentiality over clothing sale is absurdly principled.

jokes.text: Larry's dramatic protection of doctor-patient confidentiality: 'Not today, not tomorrow, not ever!'
jokes.setup: Pressure to reveal who told him about Barneys sale
jokes.punchline: Treating shopping tip like state secrets with theatrical defiance
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with theatrical delivery. Larry applying serious principles to trivial information with complete conviction.

jokes.text: I happened to overhear that conversation, and I'm very appreciative that you respected the patient-doctor confidentiality.
jokes.setup: Larry's dramatic secrecy declaration
jokes.punchline: Therapist's gratitude revealing he was the Barneys source
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff revealing the therapist as the source, validating Larry's confidentiality stance.

jokes.text: Mrs. Templeton doesn't want to meet Larry - therapist reveals wife's preference
jokes.setup: Larry asks why he can't meet Mrs. Templeton when Cheryl did
jokes.punchline: Therapist reveals his wife specifically doesn't want to meet Larry
jokes.explanation: Perfect rejection that confirms Larry's effect on people. The specificity of the wife's avoidance is brutally funny.

jokes.text: Please give my regards to Mrs. Templeton... I probably won't.
jokes.setup: Larry politely asking therapist to give regards to his wife
jokes.punchline: Therapist's honest refusal to pass along the message
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. The blunt refusal after polite request is beautifully awkward.

jokes.text: Larry asking to be called Dr. Templeton instead of Lionel after being corrected
jokes.setup: Casual conversation using first name
jokes.punchline: Professional boundary reassertion after Larry pushed too far
jokes.explanation: Perfect button on Larry's complete destruction of the professional relationship - boundaries being rebuilt.

jokes.text: Urinary tract vs fatwa seat dispute
jokes.setup: Both men claim medical need for aisle seat
jokes.punchline: Larry's fatwa fears vs other man's urinary tract problems become competing medical claims
jokes.explanation: Competing medical needs at inappropriate time. Both claims are legitimate but the priority dispute is absurd.

jokes.text: Marty acknowledges transitioning child at memorial - 'my handsome son Joey'
jokes.setup: Marty speaking at nephew's memorial
jokes.punchline: Takes moment to acknowledge and correct pronouns for transitioning child in middle of eulogy
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier transition discussion. Shows character growth and acceptance in inappropriate timing.

jokes.text: Larry shushing crying woman at memorial - tells her to cry outside
jokes.setup: Woman crying loudly during eulogy
jokes.punchline: Larry tells her to go cry outside because he can't hear
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry inappropriateness. Telling someone to stop crying at a memorial shows complete social tone-deafness.

jokes.text: Fatwa panic at memorial - Larry screams 'Fatwa! He's got a gun!' causing chaos
jokes.setup: Larry's fatwa paranoia throughout episode
jokes.punchline: Sees someone in coat and causes panic by screaming about assassination
jokes.explanation: Climactic payoff to fatwa paranoia. Disrupting memorial with false alarm is peak cringe and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Ted revelation - 'That's my friend, you idiot!' after panic
jokes.setup: Larry causes panic over perceived assassin
jokes.punchline: Person in coat turns out to be Larry's friend Ted
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of the panic. Larry's paranoia causes chaos over innocent friend. Simple but effective resolution.

jokes.text: Jeff buying the house on the spot with realtor
jokes.setup: Jeff's fake house hunting has gone on extensively
jokes.punchline: Suddenly decides to actually buy a house to continue the relationship
jokes.explanation: Escalates the dating scheme to ultimate commitment. Jeff's impulsiveness driven by sexual attraction.

jokes.text: Chair shopping scene - Larry expects therapist to pay for chair he recommended
jokes.setup: Larry finally takes therapist chair shopping as promised
jokes.punchline: Reveals he expects therapist to pay for the chair Larry picked out
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and escalation. Larry's expectation that therapist pay for his own chair improvement is peak entitlement.

tmdb_overview: Larry’s therapist accuses him of overstepping. Susie grows suspicious of Jeff. Larry bribes an usher at a funeral.
